mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment indicates slack in the labour market, which depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE level vs 20-day forward returns remains negative but insignificant (|IC|<0.02) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, the cost of capital becomes restrictive, slowing investment and consumption, leading to negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS crosses above 5%, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and payroll employment signals broad-based economic weakness, increasing recession probability and negatively impacting equities over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces 20-day forward returns that are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.1) compared to non-signal periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 1%), increases in housing starts reflect strengthening housing demand and consumer confidence, leading to short-term positive equity returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1%, the rank IC of HOUST change vs 5-day forward returns is not significantly positive over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate strong consumer demand, which boosts corporate revenues and supports equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS MoM change and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p>0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production grows at an annual rate above 3%, the supply side is accelerating, signaling robust economic activity and positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Conditional on INDPRO YoY > 3%, the median 20-day forward return is not significantly above zero (sign test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the unemployment rate, monetary policy is perceived as overly tight relative to labour market slack, creating a headwind for equities over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > UNRATE, the 20-day returns are not systematically negative (mean return > 0).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime (GDPC1 rising from trough), GDP growth momentum drives equity gains over 60 days as earnings accelerate and risk appetite improves.
failure_condition: In periods identified as post-recession recovery (NBER trough to 12 months later), the rank IC of GDPC1 quarterly change vs 60-day returns is not positive over a multi-cycle sample.
